evidence: Export privacy engine is a computation layer; export UI integration is downstream (post-pilot)
Benchmarking engine provides scoring APIs; dashboard integration is post-pilot UI work
VIP circuit breaker is a resilience layer in the existing adapter — not a new coordinator-visible feature
dod_1_coordinator_visible.notes: Sprint J defines infrastructure layers (engines, adapters, schemas). UI integration is the post-pilot phase. This is consistent with the roadmap gate: 'Solo después de datos reales de pilot.'

evidence: Export privacy masking applies when exporting subject-level data (subject_data, adverse_events report types)
No direct subject workspace UI change required — privacy engine is invoked at export time
No new action queues introduced — J1-J4 are engines/adapters, not action generators
No coordinator actions generated — external visibility layer is read-only output (exports, benchmarks, sponsor summaries)

tasks.title: Export privacy engine
tasks.notes: Role × report-type matrix (ExportRole × ExportReportType → { maskLevel, maskedFields }). maskRecord/maskRecords apply '[REDACTED]' substitution. export_audit_log table persists every export with mask_level, masked_fields[], actor_role, record_count. RLS: select + insert for active org members.

tasks.title: Sponsor visibility layer
tasks.notes: SponsorStudySummary aggregates de-identified counts only: subjectCount, enrolledCount, screenFailedCount, completedCount, activeVisitCount, missedVisitCount, openDeviationCount. No PHI exposed. 4 parallel queries via Promise.all.

tasks.title: VIP adapter hardening (circuit breaker)
tasks.notes: In-memory FSM: closed → open (after 3 failures) → half_open (after 60s recovery) → closed (on success). circuitAllows() checked at fetchWithRetry entry — blocks requests when open. circuitSuccess/circuitFailure called on every HTTP result.

tasks.title: External benchmarking / scoring
tasks.notes: 7 BenchmarkCategory values with industry references (TransCelerate 2023, CISCRP 2022, Medidata 2022). scoreAgainstBenchmark returns tier (top_quartile/median/bottom_quartile), percentileEstimate, vsMedian%, vsTopQuartile%, interpretation. buildSiteBenchmarkReport aggregates partial input into SiteBenchmarkReport.
